compartments.label: Lumen
compartments.snippet: Top region above microvilli, labeled 'Lumen', containing green bacteria, P. aeruginosa, Colonization, Toxic effectors

compartments.label: Intestinal cell
compartments.snippet: Large outer box, bottom-left labeled 'Intestinal cell', contains all signaling modules

compartments.label: Nucleus
compartments.snippet: Large interior oval (lower center/right) containing ESRE, cellular stress response genes, antimicrobial defence genes

compartments.label: Mitochondria
compartments.snippet: Organelle drawing in center module, contains mtROS, labeled UPR^mt

genes.snippet: Membrane channel/receptor icon on left signaling module, labeled TIR-1/SARM1
Yellow oval, labeled NSY-1/MAPKKK, left signaling module
Yellow oval, labeled SEK-1/MAPKK, left signaling module
Yellow oval, labeled PMK-1/p38 MAPK, left signaling module
Purple box, labeled DAF-16/FOXO, left signaling module
Purple box, labeled SKN-1/Nrf2, left signaling module
Purple box, left signaling module, adjacent to ROS box
Purple box, left signaling module, downstream of PMK-1
Blue oval in center mitochondrial module
Purple box, labeled ATFS-1/ATF-4, center mitochondria/UPR^mt pathway
Purple box, center module, downstream of AMPK; connection to AMPK marked uncertain
Purple box, center module, downstream of mtROS; linkage to HIF-1 noted as uncertain
Purple box, right module, upstream of ZIP-2; connection marked uncertain
Purple box, labeled CEBP-1/2, right module, activates ZIP-2
Purple box, right module, activates ESRE and antimicrobial defence genes
Membrane receptor icon, right side of intestinal cell; downward arrow labeled '?' toward defence genes — uncertain connection
Purple box, labeled NHR-86/HNF-4, bottom-right at nuclear/membrane edge; activates antimicrobial defence genes

tentative_edges.snippet: Pyocyanin → TIR-1/SARM1; pointed activation arrow, left pathway
Pyocyanin → NSY-1/MAPKKK; pointed activation arrow, left pathway [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
TIR-1/SARM1 → LRO stress/pH increase → feeds into MAPK cascade; inferred connection via LRO stress intermediate
NSY-1/MAPKKK → SEK-1/MAPKK; pointed arrow, left MAPK cascade
SEK-1/MAPKK → PMK-1/p38 MAPK; pointed arrow, left MAPK cascade
PMK-1/p38 MAPK → ATF-7; pointed arrow
PMK-1/p38 MAPK → DAF-16/FOXO; line linking MAPK module to DAF-16 stack, noted as uncertain [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
DAF-16/FOXO → antimicrobial defence genes (nucleus); pointed arrow
SKN-1/Nrf2 → cellular stress response genes (nucleus); pointed arrow
HSF-1 → cellular stress response genes (nucleus); pointed arrow
ATF-7 → antimicrobial defence genes (nucleus); pointed arrow
HSF-1 ↔ ROS; bidirectional or uncertain direction connection, transcription notes uncertain direction [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
Pyoverdine → +Fe³⁺/Mitochondria; dashed/pointed arrow indicating iron chelation/transport
Mitochondria → HSP-60; pointed leftward arrow in center module
HSP-60 → toward nucleus/cellular stress response; faint arrow, low confidence
UPR^mt/Mitochondria → ATFS-1/ATF-4; pointed arrow
ATFS-1/ATF-4 → cellular stress response genes (nucleus); pointed arrow
AMPK → HIF-1; pointed arrow, noted as uncertain linkage
HIF-1 → antimicrobial defence genes/ESRE (nucleus); pointed arrow
mtROS → AMPK; pointed arrow from mitochondrial ROS to AMPK [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
mtROS → AMPK; pointed arrow, mtROS is source
+Fe³⁺ → HIF-1; iron influences HIF-1, noted as uncertain
ExoA —| Translation; T-bar inhibition arrow, right pathway
Translation —| ZIP-2 or loss-of-translation relieves/induces ZIP-2; connector present, end type uncertain
ZIP-4 → ZIP-2; pointed arrow, noted as uncertain
CEBP-1/2 → ZIP-2; pointed arrow, right module
ZIP-2 → ESRE (nucleus); pointed arrow [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
ZIP-2 → antimicrobial defence genes; pointed arrow, noted as uncertain [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
FSHR-1 → defence genes; downward arrow labeled '?', dashed/uncertain — very low confidence [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
NHR-86/HNF-4 → antimicrobial defence genes; pointed arrow, bottom-right
PCN → connection uncertain, labeled '?' in figure; very low confidence
ESRE → cellular stress response genes; ESRE element inside nucleus activates stress response genes, pointed arrow [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
